clue: Smart assistant, uniquely rendered surprising historical lead, and a famous writer?
explanation: ALEXANDER HAMILTON was a smart assistant (he was Washington's presidential aide). He was uniquely rendered a surprising historical lead (he was one of the Founding Fathers, and also, more people became interested in who he was when he was the "lead" role in a historical musical. He was also a famous writer, as he wrote 51 of the Federalist Papers, and helped draft the US constitution! How does the wordplay work? ALEXA (smart assistant). NDER (uniquely "rendered" surprising = take the unique letters in rendered, and anagram them). H (historical lead). A (and a). MILTON (famous writer).
hints: The whole thing describes somebody! He was a smart assistant, was uniquely rendered a surprising historical lead, and was a famous writer!
The wordplay tells you that you can construct his name by taking the name of a smart assistant, uniquely "rendered" surprising, a historical lead, A, and the name of another famous writer.
Uniquely "rendered" surprising, is the four unique letters of "rendered", made surprising (shuffled about).
What letter can be described as the "historical lead"?
There are a million things he hasn't done, but just you wait, just you wait!
extraText: Thanks so much for trying out the longer sillier version of this puzzle. I hope you enjoyed it!
